records.title: Genesis 1:3 immediate context
records.license: Derived from the visible Bible passage
records.allowedUse: Use as local passage context only; do not turn it into a lexical or historical claim.
records.text: Genesis 1:3 presents God's speech as effective within the creation account. If the observation is about God's power, begin with the passage's own pattern: God speaks, and creation responds.

records.title: Interpreting power from Genesis 1:3
records.allowedUse: Use as a method guardrail; do not cite as factual background beyond the method principle.
records.text: A theological claim such as 'God is powerful' is strongest when it names how this text shows that truth. In Genesis 1:3, the evidence is not abstract power but God's creating command.

records.title: Cross-reference path for Genesis 1:3
records.allowedUse: Use after local context is clear; favor direct textual links over loose thematic links.
records.text: Cross-reference data commonly points from Genesis 1:3 toward passages about God's word, creation, and light. Use those links after explaining what Genesis 1 itself is doing.

records.title: Adoni-zedek in public-domain dictionary tradition
records.allowedUse: Use as background starting-point evidence; keep the claim modest and passage-first.
records.text: Public-domain dictionary tradition identifies Adoni-zedek as the king of Jerusalem in Joshua 10. The name is often connected with lord and righteousness or justice, but the passage emphasizes his response to Joshua and Gibeon.

records.title: Joshua 10 coalition context
records.license: Derived from the visible Bible passage
records.allowedUse: Use as local passage context only; do not turn it into a lexical or historical claim.
records.text: Joshua 10 frames Adoni-zedek by what he hears and does: he hears about Ai and Gibeon, fears the political and military implications, and gathers other Amorite kings against Gibeon.

records.title: Gibeon geography path
records.allowedUse: Use for geography or place-setting questions; keep location claims tentative.
records.text: For Gibeon, geography is useful because Joshua 10 turns on the city's relationship to Jerusalem, Ai, and the surrounding kings. Use location data to clarify movement and pressure, not as a stand-alone insight.

records.title: Jerusalem geography path
records.allowedUse: Use for geography or place-setting questions; keep location claims tentative.
records.text: When Jerusalem appears in Joshua 10, place data should support the narrative question: why a Jerusalem king would be concerned about Gibeon and the changing regional balance.

records.title: Cross-reference guardrail
records.allowedUse: Use as a method guardrail; do not cite as factual background beyond the method principle.
records.text: Use cross references to test and enrich an interpretation after local meaning is clear. Prefer explicit quotations, repeated wording, shared events, or strong thematic development over loose associations.
